title: Add Python AST symbol index v1
goal: Add optional deterministic symbol index with range refs where available

acceptance.assertion: The slice is implemented or explicitly closed against current main/PR state with no hidden broadening.
acceptance.verifier: PR, self-review, tests, or stop report

acceptance.assertion: RepoBrief boundaries are preserved: no hidden refresh, no Git mutation in RepoBrief core, no review/runtime correctness claim.
acceptance.verifier: PR, self-review, tests, or stop report

acceptance.assertion: Relevant tests/checks are run, or a precise blocker/gap is recorded.
acceptance.verifier: PR, self-review, tests, or stop report

acceptance.assertion: The output states what the evidence does not establish.
acceptance.verifier: PR, self-review, tests, or stop report

metadata.outcome: Implemented and verified optional deterministic Python AST symbol index v1 in Lenskit.
validation: Lenskit PR #904 CI green including pytest-full, ruff, anti-hallucination-lint, doc-freshness, parity-gate and CodeQL

verified_acceptance.rbv1-t016-scope-bound: Lenskit PR #904 adds optional Python AST symbol index v1, schema, tests and proof only; no hidden broadening into runtime or query promotion.
verified_acceptance.rbv1-t016-boundary-preserved: The index is read-only and parses source with ast; it does not import modules, execute code, mutate Git, refresh snapshots, create patches or create PRs as RepoBrief core behavior.
verified_acceptance.rbv1-t016-tests-or-gap: Targeted local tests passed and PR #904 CI was green.
verified_acceptance.rbv1-t016-non-claims: Proof and symbol index does_not_establish preserve call graph completeness, dependency completeness, runtime behavior, import success, test sufficiency, review impact and merge readiness.
